videos.title: Como Irã abateu os caças americanos? Entenda a evolução estratégica por trás da artilharia
videos.description:
O conflito no Oriente Médio atingiu um novo patamar de tensão nesta sexta-feira (03/04). O presidente do parlamento iraniano, Mohamed Galif, ironizou a queda de um caça F-15 e de uma aeronave de apoio A-10 Thunderbolt II, questionando a estratégia de Washington. Enquanto os EUA confirmam a perda do F-15 e a captura de um dos pilotos, o mistério gira em torno de como as baterias antiaéreas iranianas superaram aeronaves tão velozes e modernas. Entenda a hipótese da "assinatura de calor" e por que os radares podem ter se tornado secundários nesta guerra.

Ele disse o seguinte, abre aspas. Após derrotar o Irã 37 vezes seguidas, essa brilhante guerra sem estratégia que eles começaram agora foi rebaixada de mudança de regime para Ei, alguém pode encontrar os nossos pilotos, por favor? Uau, que progresso incrível. gênios absolutos escreveu como falamos há pouco, o Irã declarou ter derrubado um segundo avião militar dos Estados Unidos só nessa sexta-feira, autoridades americanas confirmaram a queda de um jato F15 mais cedo, mas não houve declaração, pelo menos por enquanto, da Força Aérea Americana a respeito de uma aeronave de apoio modelo A10 também ter sido atingida pelos iranianos. nós vamos apurar as possibilidades de como os dois casos podem ter acontecido. E para que a gente possa entender, primeiro vamos olhar a diferença dos eh equipamentos por enquanto, usados pelos dois países nessa guerra aérea em cima ali do território iraniano e obviamente do Golfo Pérsico. Então, uma das das hipóteses está quase que automaticamente descartada, que é o abatimento de um caça bombardeio F15 por um drone Shred 136, que virou a grande atração, a grande atenção dessa resposta iraniana aos ataques promovidos há praticamente seis semanas pelos Estados Unidos com apoio de Israel. Mas a notícia que nós temos concreta é que nessa sexta-feira houve o a derrubada de um CA bombardeio F15 com dois ocupantes, um dos pilotos americanos, porque eles conseguiram ejetar, um segue desaparecido, o outro foi capturado. Pra gente entender o que que é um F15, é um modelo já relativamente antigo, mas que ele foi modernizado ao longo das décadas. Ele opera desde 1972, ou seja, a Força Aérea Americana, ele também é usado a partir de portaaviões. Então, a Marinha e a Força Aérea dos Estados Unidos conhece muito bem esse equipamento. Mais de 1600 modelos que são construídos pela BOG já foram entregues à Força Aérea Americana, à Marinha, e estão em operações. Repito, é um equipamento muito conhecido pelos pilotos e para quem tá operando a partir dos Estados Unidos no palco de guerra. tem um raio de ação. Ele voa, ida e volta mais de 5.500 km. E o mais importante, ele tem uma velocidade de duas vezes e meia a velocidade do som, o chamado MAC 2.5. O drone Shah que mostrou a sua eficiência, principalmente porque o Iran conseguiu produzir em série e eles são muito baratos e de fácil e facilmente substituídos, podem ser usados numa operação kamicase, né? ele identifica o alvo e voa contra ele e explode nessa no impacto, ele voa a uma velocidade muito menor do que um jato desse. Então, seria incompatível uma luta aérea entre os dois. Existe uma possibilidade deste equipamento ter abatido esse? Existe, mas ela é ínfima. Então, a gente já pode descartar essa hipótese. A coisa fica com outros contornos. Eu vou pedir a próxima arte porque não é apenas o F15 em questão. Ao passar das horas nessa sexta-feira, a informação, por enquanto, a partir do Irã é que o A10 Hog também teria sido abatido e os Estados Unidos, a questão de 10 dias, confirmaram ter deslocado o Arf Hog para o palco de guerra, assim como helicópteros ache. Essas duas aeronaves não estavam no inventário original quando houve o deslocamento para a região do Oriente Médio, dos dois porta-aviões e mais os equipamentos das bases aliadas americanas na região. Este é um avião de perfil completamente diferente do F15. Ele também é um avião de combate, mas é um avião que em geral a Força Aérea a usa para apoio em terra. Ele consegue fazer manobras rápidas e voos mais rasantes em condições de apoio a eh tropas em terra, observação e também um combate à ar terra, porque ele é equipado com um fortíssimo canhão, além de capacidade de disparo de mísseis. O Irã disse também ter derrubado um desse. Talvez tenha usado um drone. Muito pouco provável, porque a capacidade de voo, de manobra e velocidade desse avião, que voa muito mais lentamente em comparação com um jato F15, ainda é muito superior de um drone. Então, como os iranianos teriam abatido essas aeronaves que tem uma teórica superioridade de voo? Uma das explicações pode tá aqui na próxima arte, que é a adaptação de sistemas antiaéreo dos iranianos que foram desenvolvidos em paralelo aqui. Os americanos tém tem tecnologia parecida e abasteceram essa tecnologia. Israel foi abastecida com essa tecnologia americana, ou seja, é algo já conhecido, que são as baterias antiaéreas, que tem um alcance de 150 a 200 km e abatem alvos numa altitude acima e na atmosfera terrestre. Estes equipamentos, eles foram produzidos originalmente para interceptação de mísseis inimigos. tem características de voo, de manobra muito diferente dos jatos que eu mostrei. Então, a regulagem original, a o setup original não previa o abatimento dessas aeronaves velozes. Porém, provavelmente, isso abre um leque de interpretação, os iranianos fizeram ajustes que mudaram essa lógica. Então, existe um conjunto de veículos no chão. O primeiro radar detecta a ameaça, o alvo é identificado e os mísseis apontados para uma trajetória de interceptação. O interceptador colide com a ameaça e aí o controle de tiro e comunicações do centro de coordenação recebe todas essas informações, processa e recalibra o sistema para um próximo disparo parecido. A questão é que, repito, esta bateria antiaérea foi projetada para derrubar míseis. Aviões como F15 tade alterar a trajetória de voo, velocidade de uma maneira muito mais eficiente do que um míssel. E alguns dos jatos, como já houve, segundo tanto os Estados Unidos quanto o Irã, a informação de que um F35 também foi abatido e o F35 tem uma característica furtiva, ele é capaz de driblar os radares. Então, como supostamente os iranianos conseguiram? A resposta tá aqui na próxima arte. Talvez há indícios a partir desta imagem que estas baterias antiaéreas foram calibradas para a assinatura de calor das aeronaves. O F35 emite uma onda de calor, o F15 outra, o A10hog outra. e que os iranianos talvez tenham aprendido a identificar o calor. Essas aeronaves, como F35 podem driblar os radares, mas eles não são capazes de eliminar o calor produzido pelos seus potentíssimos jatos. É aí que pode ter sido o calcanhar de Aquiles descoberto pelos iranianos. Ainda estamos trabalhando no campo das hipóteses.

videos.title: Análise: Irã ainda demonstra capacidade de desgastar EUA e Trump | WW
videos.description:
Duas aeronaves militares americanas foram abatidas no Irã nesta sexta-feira (3). Uma delas era um caça com dois tripulantes, um deles foi resgatado, mas não se sabe em que estado estaria, enquanto o outro ainda não foi localizado pelos militares americanos, nem pelas forças do Irã. O professor de Ciências Militares da Eceme Sandro Teixeira Moita, o professor de Ciência Política do Berea College Carlos Gustavo Poggio e o professor da NYU Shanghai e da FDC Rodrigo Zeidan comentam o assunto. #CNNBrasil

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição Duas aeronaves militares americanas foram abatidas no Irã nesta sexta-feira. Uma delas era um caça com dois tripulantes. Um deles foi resgatado pelos Estados Unidos, mas não se sabe em que estado ele estaria agora. O outro tripulante ainda não foi localizado nem pelos militares americanos e nem pelas forças do Irã. Vamos ver a reportagem de Mariana Janjá como direto de Washington. A queda de um CA F15 foi o primeiro caso registrado de uma aeronave dos Estados Unidos abatida dentro do território iraniano desde o início da guerra. Um anúncio de recompensa para quem capturasse a tripulação da aeronave foi veiculado pela mídia iraniana. Imagens divulgadas nas redes sociais e geolocalizadas pela CNN, que mostrariam operações de busca e resgate, indicam que o Caça teria caído em uma região montanhosa no sudoeste do país, próximo à planície petrolífera e à fronteira com o Iraque. Também nesta sexta-feira, a mídia do Irã reportou um comunicado do exército do país dizendo que uma outra aeronave americana caiu nas águas do Golfo Pérsico após ter sido alvejada por sistemas de defesa aérea iranianos próximo ao estreito de Ormuz. O piloto conseguiu sejetar e foi resgatado. Os Estados Unidos têm empregado o A10 Wartog no Irã para identificar alvos com precisão e realizar ataques em baixa altitude contra embarcações iranianas na região do Estreito. No pronunciamento na última quarta-feira, Donald Trump disse que o Irã já não tinha mais equipamentos antiaéreos e que os radares do país tinham sido completamente destruídos. Ele afirmou também que os mísseis iranianos tinham sido destruídos ou esgotados, mas relatórios da inteligência sinalizam desafios um mês depois de bombardeios diários junto a Israel. Fontes com acesso às informações destes documentos disseram a CNN que a inteligência americana aponta que cerca de metade dos drones e dos lançadores de mísseis do Irã seguem intactos. Um dos desafios para os Estados Unidos é que o Irã tem levado lançadores para o subsolo, escondendo-os em túneis e cavernas, mas ainda mantendo-os operacionais. Embora as capacidades da marinha oficial do Irã tenham sido praticamente destruídas, a força naval ligada à Guarda Revolucionária, que tem capitaneado o bloqueio iraniano sobre o estreito de Ormus, ainda conservaria metade de suas capacidades, também segundo estes relatórios. Teriam sido os sistemas de média altura e de grande altura. Mas o Trump não é fã de dizer que tem superioridade aérea, obviamente saiu dizendo isso. O professor de Ciências Militares da Eceme Sandro Teixeira Moita, o professor de Ciência Política do Berea College Carlos Gustavo Poggio e o professor da NYU Shanghai e da FDC Rodrigo Zeidan comentam o assunto. #CNNBrasil

videos.title: Irã divulga imagens bombásticas de helicóptero CH-47 Chinook americano destruído após impacto dir...
videos.description:
A mídia estatal iraniana divulgou imagens não verificadas alegando que um ataque com drone danificou um helicóptero CH-47 Chinook dos EUA no Kuwait. Relatos sugerem que a aeronave fazia parte de uma missão relacionada à queda de um caça F-15E Strike Eagle dos EUA em território iraniano. Um membro da tripulação teria sido resgatado, enquanto outro permanece desaparecido, e as operações de busca continuam. O Pentágono não confirmou as alegações. Com o aumento das tensões, o presidente dos EUA, Donald Trump, classificou a situação como "guerra", sinalizando que não haverá trégua nas hostilidades. Os acontecimentos representam uma escalada perigosa no conflito em curso entre Irã e EUA.

#GuerraIrãEUA #AtaqueAoChinook #F15Abatido #ÚltimasNotícias #ConflitoNoOrienteMédio #ForçasMilitarEUA #AtaqueAoIrã #AlertaDeGuerra #Geopolítica #Trump

O Times of India (TOI) é o jornal diário em inglês mais vendido do mundo.

Os vídeos do Times of India trazem notícias globais, opiniões e análises precisas. Acompanhamos a ascensão global da Índia, seu crescente engajamento com o mundo, o cenário geopolítico em transformação em meio a conflitos e guerras e a ordem mundial emergente.

NOTÍCIAS INTERNACIONAIS | CONFLITOS GLOBAIS | GUERRA NO ORIENTE MÉDIO | MUDANÇAS NA ORDEM MUNDIAL #TOILive | #TOIVideos

Inscreva-se no canal do Times of India no YouTube e clique no ícone do sino para receber notificações quando entrarmos ao vivo.

Junte-se a milhões de leitores do TOI; baixe nosso aplicativo em http://toi.in/app-yt

Siga-nos no X/ @timesofindia
Facebook:   / timesofindia  
Instagram:   / timesofindia  
Siga o canal do TOI no WhatsApp: https://bit.ly/3RYl0J9
Para notícias e atualizações diárias e reportagens exclusivas, siga o Times of India
videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição A dramatic escalation in the Iran US conflict. Unverified images now claim a direct Iranian strike on a US military helicopter in Q8. Iranian state media outlet Press TV has released these images purportedly showing a damaged US Army CH47 Shinook at Camp Buring, a key American military hub in the Gulf. According to Iranian reports, a drone strike hit the aircraft, leaving the heavy lift helicopter severely damaged. The Shinook is a critical asset used to move troops, weapons, and supplies across the region. But there is a major catch. There is no official confirmation yet from Washington. The Pentagon has remained silent so far. Another Iranian outlet claims something even more striking. It says the helicopter was part of a search operation looking for a downed US fighter jet pilot. Because behind this incident lies an even bigger story. A US F-15E Strike Eagle has been shot down over Iran, marking a major turning point in the conflict. According to reports, one crew member has been rescued alive and now in US custody, receiving medical treatment, but the second crew member is still missing. Search and rescue operations are ongoing and time is critical. Meanwhile, tensions continue to surge. US President Donald Trump has responded bluntly, saying the loss of the aircraft will not affect diplomacy. His words were direct. This is war. Trump has also declined to reveal how the US would respond if the missing crew member is captured or harmed. That silence is raising new questions about what comes next. A major development is now emerging from the Iran war, marking a significant escalation in direct confrontation between the United States and Iran. According to American media, it is now confirmed that a US fighter jet was shot down over Iran on Friday. US special forces have located one of the crew members. Multiple reports quoting sources say the aircraft involved was an F-15E Strike Eagle, a twin seat fighter jet used for both airtoair and airto ground missions. The jet was carrying two crew members, a pilot and a weapons systems officer. Reportedly, both crew members successfully ejected after the aircraft was hit by Iranian fire. While one person was rescued alive from inside Iranian territory, search operations are still ongoing for the second crew member. In a significant operational decision, Israel reportedly cancelled planned strikes inside Iran to avoid interfering with the ongoing rescue mission. Iranian state media was the first to report that the US jet had been shot down, though it claimed the aircraft was F-35. State television in Iran also broadcast a message saying that anyone who locates the US personnel would be rewarded by the government. This marks the first confirmed instance since the start of the war that a US fighter jet has been downed by Iran's fire. The White House has acknowledged the development. Press Secretary Caroline Levit told Axios that the president has been briefed on the situation. The F-15E Strike Eagle manufactured by Boeing is a key component of US air power. It is designed to carry out deep strike missions as well as engage in aerial combat. This incident comes amid mounting losses in the conflict. Earlier in the war, three F-15E aircraft were reportedly lost due to friendly fire. The human cost is also rising. At least 13 American personnel have been killed since the start of the campaign. This comes after Donald Trump and his defense secretary Pete Hegseath have both repeatedly suggested that the US has effectively won this war. Hegsathth and military commanders have spoken of air superiority and how degraded Iran's air defenses are. This shows that Iran still retains capacity to defend its skies. As the search continues for the missing crew member and tensions remain at a peak, this latest incident underscores the growing risks facing US forces in an increasingly dangerous and expanding conflict. Iran downs US fighter jet captures its pilot amid war. US military's frantic search for captured pilot fails. Choppers deployed to trace missing F-35 fighter pilot. After Iran shot down a US F-35 fighter jet on Friday, a video circulated on social media showing American helicopters and planes searching for the missing pilot in southern Iran's Kistan. Iranian media also urged residents to capture the pilot dead or alive for a promising reward. Given that the American F-35 fighter J of the Zionist regime, the provincial police force issued an urgent statement announcing that an American Zionist enemy F-35 fighter jet had been targeted and subsequently shot down within the nation's airspace in the central region of the country. And given the potential for the pilot or pilots of this fighter jet to land in Kyiluier and Buoyer Ahmmed province, we earnestly request all members of the general public, including our esteemed nomadic communities and villagers to extend their full cooperation to the military and law enforcement agencies. To the esteemed residents of Kiluya and Buer Ahmad province, you, our beloved, honorable and fiercely patriotic people, I urge you, should you manage to apprehend any enemy pilot or pilots and successfully deliver them alive and unharmed into the custody of our military and law enforcement agencies, you will receive a valuable reward and a bonus for good news. Iranian IRGC linked Tasnim news agency claimed that Iranian forces foiled the US military's bid to rescue the downed fighter jet pilot. Iran's farce news agency shared video showing a purported missile attack on an F-15 air base in Israel's Hifa. The Iranian air defense systems also claimed to have successfully downed an American UH60 Blackhawk helicopter in Iraqi airspace, but the reports remain unverified. Later, Iran's Tasnim news agency claimed that the US military was trying to extract the fighter jet pilot whose condition remained unclear. The operation involved Blackhawk helicopters and a C130 Hercules aircraft. However, their efforts have so far been futile, the agency said. Washington has not commented on any of the claims regarding the alleged downing of a jet or the rescue mission. In March, an American F-35 had to execute an emergency landing after being struck by Iranian fire. Iran has been carrying out retaliatory strikes on US and Israeli military installations in the Middle East. Meanwhile, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Friday vowed to crush Iran. Together with our American friends, we are continuing to dismantle the terror regime in Iran. We are eliminating commanders. We are bombing uh bridges and we are striking their infrastructure. In recent days, our air force has successfully destroyed 70% of Iran's steel production capacity. This is a truly significant achievement that effectively deprivives the revolutionary guards of both crucial financial resources and their capability to manufacture uh numerous weapons. In in full coordination between me and President Trump, between the Israel Defense Forces and the United States Army, we will continue to crush Iran. This regime is weaker than ever. Israel is stronger than ever. Iran has claimed a big strike on United States air force capabilities, having downed nine fighter jets. Iran cripples Trump's air power. Fighter jets downed. F-15, three in Kuwait, one in Thran. F-16, one in central region, one in south, F18, one in south, F-35, one in south, one in central region. The US Central Command in a statement rejected IRGC's claims saying, and I quote, "All US fighter aircraft are accounted for. Iran's IRGC has made the same false claim at least half a dozen times." The IRGC's claim comes amid Secretary of War asking the Army's top uniformed officer, General Randy George, to step down as the United States wages a war against Iran. George has been asked to take early retirement from the post of Army Chief of Staff which he has held since August 2023. The development comes as a new report claimed that Trump administration officials were hiding the data related to US troops wounded or killed in the Middle East.

videos.title: Hezbollah destrói tanques israelenses enquanto a frente no norte de Israel enfrenta o colapso.
videos.description:
Hezbollah destrói tanques israelenses enquanto a frente no norte de Israel enfrenta colapso

O Hezbollah divulgou imagens reivindicando ataques diretos a posições militares israelenses no sul do Líbano e nas Colinas de Golã ocupadas, incluindo um tanque Merkava e uma base importante. O vídeo também destaca o crescente uso de drones FPV, o aumento das perdas militares israelenses e a expansão da frente norte, enquanto as tensões regionais continuam a se intensificar em vários teatros de operações.

#Hezbollah #Israel #Líbano #ColinasDeGolã #Merkava #IDF #OrienteMédio #ConfrontosNaFronteira #ÚltimasNotícias #AtualizaçãoMilitar
#NotíciasInternacionais
videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição What is unfolding on Israel's northern front is increasingly being described as one of the most active and complex parts of the wider regional confrontation. New visuals released by Hezbollah are being presented as evidence of two separate operations, one along southern Lebanon and another in the occupied Syrian Golan, reinforcing the group's message that it remains active across more than one theater at the same time. The material is being circulated at a moment when pressure is already rising across several fronts and when both sides are trying not only to shape the battlefield but also the public narrative around it. In the first set of visuals, Hezbollah says it targeted an Israeli Macaba tank in Albiada in southern Lebanon using what it described as an Almasguided anti-armour missile. The footage appears to show a munition moving toward an armored vehicle position near a builtup area before an impact is seen on screen. However, as is often the case with wartime footage released by armed groups or military organizations, the full outcome cannot be independently confirmed from the video alone. There has been no immediate public Israeli confirmation matching the exact details of that incident and that remains an important point when assessing claims tied to rapidly released battlefield material. In the second release, Hezbollah published footage it said showed missile launches aimed at the Ka base in the occupied Syrian Golden Heights. That claim, if accurate, would further support the idea that Hezbollah is seeking to project operational reach beyond the immediate Lebanon border zone and into a broader pressure campaign. The group has repeatedly framed its activities as part of a multiffront effort. And these latest releases appear designed to reinforce that image. Even when specific battlefield outcomes remain unclear, the messaging value of such footage is significant. It signals capability, persistence, and intent, especially at a time when military and political pressure is building throughout the region. The system itself has become an increasingly discussed part of Hezbollah's battlefield messaging. Analysts tracking the group's media releases say the missile has appeared more frequently in footage involving armored targets, particularly in southern Lebanon. It is widely described as a precisiong guided anti-armour system, and its repeated appearance in Hezbollah releases suggests the group wants viewers to associate it with tactical effectiveness against protected vehicles. Whether every claimed hit produces the level of battlefield effect implied in the videos is harder to determine, but the recurring use of the system in media releases clearly serves a strategic communication purpose. At the same time, another element is becoming more central to the confrontation firsterson view or FPV drones. These are smaller systems than the larger reconnaissance or one-way drones more commonly associated with earlier phases of regional escalation. According to reporting cited by Israeli media, Hezbollah is increasingly turning to FPV style systems that can be guided in real time and used with a high degree of precision. In some cases, military observers say such platforms can be adapted to resist electronic interference, including by using fiber optic links or other methods that reduce vulnerability to jamming. That matters because electronic defense has long been one of the core tools used to counter drone threats. Recently circulated footage has shown these small systems moving low and fast toward Israeli armored positions. Their compact size, maneuverability, and relative affordability make them difficult to compare with older battlefield tools. What once might have required heavier fire support can now in some cases be attempted with a portable and highly flexible system. Military analysts often compare this shift to the lessons seen in the Russia Ukraine conflict where FPV drones rapidly changed the way frontline units had to think about movement, exposure, armor protection, and even logistics. That same adaptation now appears to be influencing tactics in southern Lebanon. For Israeli forces operating near the border, this creates a layered threat environment. Traditional rocket fire remains a factor. Guided anti-armour systems remain a factor. Ambushes and terrain shaped by hills, villages, vegetation, and damaged infrastructure remain a factor. And now smaller precisiong guided drones add another constant risk. The result is a battlefield where even brief movement can carry heightened exposure. This is one reason why military assessments increasingly describe the northern front not simply as an exchange zone, but as a persistent attritional theater where technology, terrain, and timing all matter at once. Israeli authorities have also reported a rise in military losses and injuries during the recent phase of operations. Various Israeli military and medical updates have pointed to a steady stream of admissions and casualties linked to the ongoing confrontation in the north. In one 24-hour period, Israeli military reporting indicated that dozens of soldiers had been wounded. Other reports have referred to fatalities among active duty personnel, including members of the NAL brigade as well as reserve forces affected in separate incidents. Some of these figures have been repeated across multiple reports, but exact totals can shift quickly as additional information emerges, so any specific number should be treated as part of a moving battlefield picture rather than a final ledger. Israeli military statements have also described the challenges faced by forces operating in contested areas of southern Lebanon. Those challenges include Hezbollah's mobility, the use of mixed tactics, the ability to shift between indirect fire and close-range engagements, and the continued threat posed by guided systems aimed at armored vehicles. Israeli assessment suggests that the environment is not only physically demanding, but tactically fluid. Units may be dealing with rockets in one moment, a drone in the next, and then an anti-armour engagement or ambush shortly afterward. That kind of sequencing can increase operational pressure even when individual incidents appear limited in isolation. In one recent cluster of incidents, Israeli reporting indicated that four soldiers, including an officer, were lost in southern Lebanon, while others were injured, including at least one in serious condition. In other cases, the Israel Defense Forces said one soldier was moderately injured after shrapnel from a rocket landed near troops while three reser sustained light injuries after a drone detonated close to their position. All were evacuated for treatment and the military said families had been informed even when the immediate physical impact is limited. The repeated nature of such incidents matters. but underscores how sustained pressure can build over time and force constant adjustments on the ground. The broader public effect inside Israel is also visible. Funerals, public mourning, and emotional appeals from families have become part of the atmosphere surrounding the northern confrontation. One especially painful moment came during remarks at a funeral following a Hezbollah anti-armour incident. The speaker said, "Oh my pure, precious soul, I was blessed to have you, even if for just a short while. And I want to thank the Holy One. Blessed be he who gave you to us, even though it was for such a brief time. May your soul in heaven watch over all of Israel. May we finally receive good news. Enough of this pain. Children lying here in the ground. What are all these lives worth? What is life worth? Enough of wars. Please make peace already.

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição Israel needs Lebanon to defeat Hezbollah, but the Israelis are exhausted by all of this. Officials with the IDF reported that they are unable to disarm Hezbollah as it would require a near total occupation of Lebanon. They also reported that the Lebanese government was the only government cap currently capable of disarmament with Harets reporting that later on Friday the IDF stated it was committed to the long-term goal of disarming Hezbollah. The goal includes a wide range of efforts that will continue over time, the IDF said, adding that the current round of fighting weakens Hezbollah and will advance the achievement of this goal over time. But how much time will the people of Israel afford their government? As Israel begins its territorial expansion into Lebanon, attempting to hedge all the way up to the Latani River, people in Israel appear to be growing weary with the with the shifting goalposts. On Tuesday, dozens of parents whose children serve in the Nahal Brigade's ren reconnaissance battalion urged Netanyahu to reconsider their son's deployment in southern Lebanon, saying it puts them at unreasonable risk. After a Passover spent darting between various shelters, many seem exhausted with the direction of the conflict war. When the war began, is Israelis were overwhelmingly supportive of the war effort. For the first two weeks of the war with Iran, polls found that over 80% of Israelis support the war, driven mainly by over 90% support among Jewish respondents in multiple polls from think tanks. About twothirds of Israel's Arab Arab Palestinian citizens opposed the war according to the Israel Democracy Institute surveys and just one quarter supported it. But almost immediately show signs of fatigue started to show. By the second week, there were small signs of doubt such as a slight decline in the portion of Jewish respondents who strongly supported the war according to the IDI polling. Now by the fourth week of the war, total support has declined from 83% at first in IDI studies to 68% who support continuing the war. That means support is still overwhelming, but a 15point drop in just four weeks is still a seismic shift. And Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is much less popular than the war effort itself. Just 36% give give him high or moderate trust levels among all Israelis in the fourth fourth week of the war with no wartime boost. The rest do not trust him. Even among Jewish Israelis, 56% don't trust him. And of four possible responses, the largest portion, 45% of all Israelis do not trust him at all. Brett, a lot of this takes buyin and support from the population. They had that for the most part with Gaza. This is a different story and support is cratering fast. What does this look like in the next month or two? I don't know. I'm not in the minds or perspective of people in Israel or southern Lebanon or anywhere, but I do know that generally support for wars waines when you see the real price of it. And when the real price is like people in your family who have to um you know execute the orders of a leader you don't trust will then support Waynees. But I understand like I think probably what the situation is is people in America don't fully understand what it's like to live in any of these areas um and don't have the same relationship with uh the zone. I mean definitely don't have a relationship with the zone that um that the people there do. And so I understand that in the wake of October 7th you're going to have like a huge support for doing things to make sure that that never ever happens again. And the further you get from essentially uh that moment and once you find yourself sending your kid to southern Lebanon to try to get Hezbollah to stop being um along with all of this like that's it's very difficult cuz every kind of conflict that you have I mean people like to just pick a start date or pick a side and then pick a start date but you can go back and back and back and back and back and the more you do that the more you think about it the more enraged you get and there's lots of reasons to get enraged and everybody I should learn as much as possible um and experience that frustration um to try to get to a solution. But I just don't think that anyone really is looking around and saying like from the American perspective us bombing and invading Iran seems like a really cool easy way to win a war soon. And as support for it waines, I mean just generally what I have observed as trying to learn as much as I can about these this situation and this this part of the map is like there is a thing that happens uh people die. Um Israel then goes and kills more people than were killed in Israel and America says I got to hold you back. Stop. And then there is some kind of thing that happens where it stops. Now, there's going to be lasting things that affect the map after that. For example, like the occupation of uh the West Bank, for example, no longer there being Israeli forces in the Sinai Peninsula. Like the maps change, but usually I understand that like I understand that like if you're Israel, you're beset on all sides by enemies. if you're the people Israel considers enemies, like that used to be your people's land up until some imperialist shift changed the situation on the ground. Um, and something I don't think American observers definitely for the most part can really wrap their mind around is like finding some very tense temporary peace being a win. That's my thought. What about you, Jordan? Engage those thoughts. Yeah. I mean, I think the reason we're seeing support plummet so fast is it's not just a one-sided conflict. It's definitely lopsided, but it's not one-sided in the way that Israel's genocide in Gaza was. They weren't living under content constant threat of legitimate retaliatory attacks like they are now from both Iran and Lebanon. And if the reporting that we're seeing is true about some of this missile defense, some of these missile defense systems not intercepting missiles, uh, potentially system failure in some parts of the country, that is going to be understandably unnerving for the people who are in those areas. And the damage from these retaliatory strikes is much more severe than anything we saw Hamas do outside of October 7th. So it's much harder to sell your base or sell your country and your population on a new war when within the first couple weeks they're already starting to experience firsthand those consequences. It's easier to sell Americans on any sort of conflict because for the most part with the exception of like Pearl Harbor, it's just not here. I guess you could count 911, but like with that with th those two exceptions, those aren't waged here. So, we don't have to live under constant stress or fear or worry of, you know, having to scramble in the middle of the night to a bomb shelter or take refuge. That's that's something that many people in throughout that region are constantly living under. I have got a buddy in Jordan right now who was saying he he heard bomb sirens and didn't know where to go earlier today. So, it's it's stressful and it's harder to sell a war when it's not one-sided. Every time you ring the bell below, an angel gets his wings. Totally not true. But it does keep you updated on our live shows.

videos.title: Bloqueio de Bab al-Mandab em seguida? Irã lança ameaça bombástica em meio ao fechamento de Ormuz ...
videos.description:
O Irã sinalizou uma possível escalada ao insinuar ameaças ao Estreito de Bab el-Mandeb, uma importante rota marítima global. O presidente do Parlamento, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, questionou o volume de petróleo, GNL e bens essenciais que passam por esse ponto estratégico, uma medida amplamente vista como um aviso. A hidrovia movimenta uma parcela significativa do comércio global e do fornecimento de energia, sendo crucial para o comércio entre a Europa e a Ásia. Analistas acreditam que Teerã pode estar expandindo sua estratégia de pressão para além do Estreito de Ormuz, especialmente com o apoio de grupos aliados como os Houthis, aumentando as preocupações com uma possível interrupção mais ampla das cadeias de suprimentos globais.

#AmeaçaIrã #BabElMandeb #ComércioGlobal #CriseDeOrmuz #TensõesNoOrienteMédio #FornecimentoDePetróleo #CriseNavegação

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição Iran has hinted at blocking another global choke point with the help of its allies in the Middle East. The speaker of Iran's parliament, Muhammad Bagghar Galibath, has issued a veiled threat. In a post on [music] X, Galibah questioned the vulnerability of the Babel Elmandeb Strait. He asked, "What share of global oil, LNG, wheat, rice, and fertilizer shipments passes through this route, and which [music] countries and companies depend on it the most?" A statement widely seen as a strategic warning [music] to global shipping and trade networks. While the straight of Hormuz remains the world's most critical energy corridor, the Babel Mandeb straight [music] is also vital. The narrow waterway bordered by Yemen handles nearly 14% [music] of global trade shipments. That includes around 5% of [music] the world's oil supply. Analysts say the remarks suggest Tehran may be looking beyond Hormuz and considering other strategic pressure points in [music] the global commerce network. Gibbove questioned the volume of crude oil, LNG, grains, [music] and fertilizers that pass through the narrow waterway. A statement quickly [music] picked up by policy makers and global markets as a calculated strategic [music] signal. Around 6.2 2 million barrels of crude oil pass through this route every day and nearly 12% of [music] global trade moves across the Red Sea corridor making it a critical artery for Europe [music] Asia trade and global supply chains. Its vulnerability has been a growing concern especially since the Houthi movement [music] entered the conflict in support of Iran. With tensions already high in the Gulf, this latest signal suggests the conflict [music] could expand to yet another critical maritime lifeline. A war of narratives is now intensifying alongside the battlefield as both Iran and the United States project sharply contrasting claims about the ongoing conflict. In a post on X, Ibrahim Razi, spokesman for Iran's Parliament National Security Block, drew a direct comparison between Iranian actions and what he described as failed USIsraeli objectives. Rzai claimed that Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy has taken control of the Strait of Hormuz while accusing the United States and Israel of failing to occupy even a single meter of Iranian territory. He concluded with a warning saying that it is ultimately the battlefield that will determine the outcome of the war. Meanwhile, Donald Trump has signaled a more aggressive US posture on the straight of Hormuz. In a post on Truth Social, Trump said, "With a little more time, we can easily open the Hormuz Straight, take the oil, and make a fortune. It would be a gusher for the world. However, the statement did not clarify how the United States would break Iran's hold over the waterway or what specific oil resources were being referenced. The straight of Hormuz remains a critical global energy choke point, handling a significant share of the world's oil shipments. Since the start of the conflict on February 28th, the route has been largely shut down, disrupting shipping flows from countries including Greece, the UAE, and China. While Trump has claimed the United States is less affected due to limited dependence on the route, fuel prices have surged sharply. In the US, average gas prices have risen by more than 30% in a month, crossing the $4 per gallon mark. Beyond Hormuz, experts are now warning of the long-term financial strain of the war on the United States with concerns growing over unmet strategic objectives. According to a Reuters report citing five sources familiar with US intelligence, only about onethird of Iran's missile and drone arsenal has been destroyed so far. Another third is believed to be damaged or hidden in underground tunnels and bunkers, leaving Iran with a significant remaining stockpile. This assessment contradicts earlier claims by Trump who said Iran had very few rockets left and other US statements suggesting its missile capabilities had been largely eliminated. Iran considers its ballistic missile program a key deterrent given the military superiority of the United States in Israel. Over the past month, Iranian missiles and drones have continued to strike Israel and key infrastructure in the Gulf, causing significant economic disruption. Trump himself acknowledged the risks, saying even if 99% of missiles are neutralized, 1% is unacceptable because that one missile could hit a billion dollar ship. Israel says it has targeted Iran's missile launchers extensively, claiming to have neutralized around 335 launchers, or roughly 70% of its total. However, Iran has dispersed its launch systems across the country, making detection and destruction more difficult. While the number of launches has decreased, missiles continue to penetrate defenses as interceptor stockpiles are increasingly stretched. A US official speaking anonymously to Reuters news agency admitted, "I don't know if we'll ever have an accurate number of Iran's missile stockpile." At the same time, the financial cost of the war is mounting rapidly. According to a Wall Street Journal report, US military equipment losses in the first 3 weeks alone are estimated between 1.4 and $2.9 billion. The report citing former Pentagon official Ela McCusker details multiple incidents. Three F-15E fighter jets were mistakenly shot down by a Kuwaiti aircraft. An F-35 made an emergency landing after reportedly coming under fire and a mid-air collision between two KC135 refueling planes killed six crew members. Five additional KC135 aircraft were damaged in an Iranian missile strike in Saudi Arabia, while more than a dozen MQ9 Reaper drones have been lost during the conflict. Iranian strikes have also damaged aircraft and radar systems at US bases in Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Jordan. A fire aboard the aircraft carrier USS Gerald Ford has forced it into repairs in Greece. The Pentagon is now seeking a massive $200 billion supplemental budget to replenish losses and sustain operations. Separately, a joint analysis by the Center for Strategic and International Studies and the BBC estimates that Iranian strikes caused around $800 million in damage to US bases in just the first two weeks of the war. Much of this damage occurred during Iran's initial retaliatory strikes following US-Israeli attacks. The analysis also highlights damage to advanced THA defense systems with some components reportedly redeployed from South Korea to the Middle East. According to defense briefings, the first six days of the war cost the US 11.3 billion, rising to $16.5 billion within 12 days. With additional funding requests now under consideration, the total cost of the conflict is expected to rise significantly. As the war drags on, it is becoming increasingly clear that beyond the battlefield, the economic, strategic, and geopolitical costs are mounting on all sides.

videos.title: Irã bombardeia estação da CIA e casa de alto diplomata americano com precisão cirúrgica | Grande ...
videos.description:
Os danos causados ​​à embaixada dos EUA em Riad, na Arábia Saudita, pelos ataques iranianos foram maiores do que o divulgado. Segundo uma reportagem da mídia americana, o ataque noturno envolveu dois drones que invadiram o complexo da embaixada, atingindo uma área de segurança onde centenas de funcionários trabalham. A sede da Agência Central de Inteligência (CIA) estaria entre os locais atingidos. Outros drones foram interceptados horas depois, com destroços caindo perto de uma escola infantil e um drone tendo como alvo a residência de um importante diplomata americano.

#iranwar2026 #iran #middleeastconflict #usiranwarupdates #iranwar #usembassy #saudiarabia

videos.title: EM CÂMERA: Irã abate caça F-16 israelense após atingir 4 jatos e helicópteros americanos?
videos.description:
A agência de notícias iraniana Tasnim informou que um caça supostamente ligado aos Estados Unidos ou a Israel pode ter sido abatido, citando imagens de destroços da aeronave obtidas por seu repórter. Segundo a reportagem, um correspondente militar avaliou que os destroços visíveis incluem um único escapamento de motor, sugerindo que a aeronave poderia ser um F-16 ou possivelmente um F-35, sendo o F-16 considerado mais provável. A alegação não foi verificada de forma independente e não houve confirmação oficial das autoridades iranianas ou de outras fontes.

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição Iran's Tausnim news agency has reported that a fighter jet allegedly linked to the United States or Israel may have been shot down, citing images of aircraft debris obtained by its reporter. According to the report, a military correspondent assessed that the visible wreckage includes a single engine exhaust, suggesting the aircraft could be an F-16 or possibly an F-35, with the F-16 considered more likely. Taznim also stated that several fighter jets were observed flying over cities including Thrron where Iranian air defense units reportedly engaged them. Some of the aircraft were said to have deployed flares while attempting to evade the fire. The claim has not been independently verified and there has been no official confirmation from Iranian authorities or other sources. A second US military aircraft incident. According to a report by the New York Times, an A10 Warthog attack aircraft has crashed in the region. The incident took place near the straight of Hormuz, one of the world's most critical and volatile maritime choke points currently. US officials told the New York Times that the aircraft went down on Friday around the same time another major incident unfolded. Meanwhile, an F-15E Strike Eagle was shot down over Iran, marking a serious escalation in the ongoing conflict. Reports say one crew member has been rescued so far, while search operations continue for the second. In contrast, the A10 crash involved a single pilot who was successfully rescued. However, key questions remain unanswered. US officials have provided limited details on what caused the crash and the exact location of the incident. The A10 Warthog, known for its close air support role, is designed to operate in high-risk combat zones, often flying low and directly over battlefields. And now with two US aircraft incidents unfolding almost simultaneously, concerns are mounting over the risks facing American air operations in an increasingly volatile region. A major development is now emerging from the Iran war, marking a significant escalation in direct confrontation between the United States and Iran. According to American media, it is now confirmed that a US fighter jet was shot down over Iran on Friday. US special forces have located one of the crew members. Multiple reports quoting sources say the aircraft involved was an F-15E Strike Eagle, a twin seat fighter jet used for both airtoair and airto ground missions. The jet was carrying two crew members, a pilot and a weapons systems officer. Reportedly, both crew members successfully ejected after the aircraft was hit by Iranian fire. While one person was rescued alive from inside Iranian territory, search operations are still ongoing for the second crew member. In a significant operational decision, Israel reportedly cancelled planned strikes inside Iran to avoid interfering with the ongoing rescue mission. Iranian state media was the first to report that the US jet had been shot down, though it claimed the aircraft was F-35. State television in Iran also broadcast a message saying that anyone who locates the US personnel would be rewarded by the government. This marks the first confirmed instance since the start of the war that a US fighter jet has been downed by Iran's fire. The White House has acknowledged the development. Press Secretary Caroline Levitt told Axios that the president has been briefed on the situation. The F-15E Strike Eagle manufactured by Boeing is a key component of US air power. It is designed to carry out deep strike missions as well as engage in aerial combat. This incident comes amid mounting losses in the conflict. Earlier in the war, three F-15E aircraft were reportedly lost due to friendly fire. The human cost is also rising. At least 13 American personnel have been killed since the start of the campaign. This comes after Donald Trump and his defense secretary Pete Hegsth have both repeatedly suggested that the US has effectively won this war. Hegsathth and military commanders have spoken of air superiority and how degraded Iran's air defenses are. This shows that Iran still retains capacity to defend its skies. Given that the American F-35 fighter J of the Zionist regime, the provincial police force issued an urgent statement announcing that an American Zionist enemy F-35 fighter jet had been targeted and subsequently shot down within the nation's airspace in the central region of the country. And given the potential for the pilot or pilots of this fighter jet to land in Kiluier and Boyer Ahmmed province, we earnestly request all members of the general public, including our esteemed nomadic communities and villages to extend their full cooperation to the military and law enforcement agencies. To the esteemed residents of Kiluyuer and Buer Ahmad province, you, our beloved, honorable and fiercely patriotic people, I urge you, should you manage to apprehend any enemy pilot or pilots and successfully deliver them alive and unharmed into the custody of our military and law enforcement agencies. The Iranian air defense systems also claimed to have successfully downed an American UH60 Blackhawk helicopter in Iraqi airspace, but the reports remain unverified. Later, Iran's Tasnim news agency claimed that the US military was trying to extract the fighter jet pilot whose condition remained unclear. The operation involved Blackhawk helicopters and a C130 Hercules aircraft. However, their efforts have so far been futile, the agency said. Washington has not commented on any of the claims regarding the alleged downing of a jet or the rescue mission. In March, an American F-35 had to execute an emergency landing after being struck by Iranian fire. Iran has been carrying out retaliatory strikes on US and Israeli military installations in the Middle East. Meanwhile, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Friday wowed to crush Iran. Together with our American friends, we are continuing to dismantle the terror regime in Iran. We are eliminating commanders. We are bombing uh bridges and we are striking their infrastructure. In recent days, our air force has successfully destroyed 70% of Iran's steel production capacity. This is a truly significant achievement that effectively deprivives the revolutionary guards of both crucial financial resources and their capability to manufacture uh numerous weapons. In in full coordination between me and President Trump, between the Israel Defense Forces and the United States Army, we will continue to crush Iran. This regime is weaker than ever. Israel is stronger than ever. Iran has claimed a big strike on United States Air Force capabilities, having downed nine fighter jets. Iran cripples Trump's air power. Fighter jets downed. F-15, three in Kuwait, one in Thran. F-16, one in central region, one in south, F18, one in south, F-35, one in south, one in central region. The US Central Command in a statement rejected IRGC's claims saying, and I quote, "All US fighter aircraft are accounted for. Iran's IRGC has made the same false claim at least half a dozen times." The IRGC's claim comes amid Secretary of War asking the Army's top uniformed officer, General Randy George, to step down as the United States wages a war against Iran. videos.title: Um dos dois tripulantes do F-15E foi resgatado: Coronel aposentado da Força Aérea dos EUA detalha...
videos.description:
Os EUA resgataram um militar de um caça F-15E abatido sobre o Irã, disseram fontes americanas à CNN. A situação do segundo tripulante não estava clara imediatamente após os EUA iniciarem as buscas no país.

O Irã afirmou que suas forças armadas abateram o caça americano. A mídia estatal pediu aos iranianos que ficassem atentos à tripulação americana e prometeu uma recompensa por sua entrega.

0:00 Fontes: 1 de 2 tripulantes resgatados após caça abatido no Irã
0:19 O analista militar da CNN, Coronel da Força Aérea dos EUA Cedric Leighton (aposentado), explica as imagens dos destroços
5:53 O analista militar da CNN, Tenente-General Mark Schwartz (aposentado), comenta o que provavelmente está acontecendo no Quartel-General do CENTCOM
8:44 Schwartz descreve o treinamento de sobrevivência militar
11:23 Trump está exagerando o impacto dos ataques dos EUA no Irã?
13:43 O painel do programa "Inside Politics" analisa a opinião pública sobre a guerra com o Irã.

Assista a mais vídeos da CNN aqui: https://cnn.it/4kh5RPe

#GuerraNoIrã #Caça #Notícias

videos.title: Haverá uma invasão terrestre do Irã? (com o Coronel Larry Wilkerson) | The Chris Hedges Report

Enquanto os EUA se preparam para intensificar a guerra contra o Irã com uma possível invasão terrestre, o Coronel Wilkerson revela a longa relutância dos planejadores militares americanos em confrontar o Irã e a insensatez de tal empreendimento.

videos.title: A surpreendente estratégia de guerra do Irã - Robinson Farinazzo - Programa 20 Minutos
videos.description:
A surpreendente estratégia de guerra do Irã - Robinson Farinazzo - Programa 20 Minutos

🌍 Neste episódio urgente do Programa 20 Minutos, dissecamos a mais nova e ousada jogada do tabuleiro geopolítico global: o fechamento do Estreito de Hormuz pelo Irã como resposta direta à guerra declarada por Donald Trump contra a nação persa em fevereiro de 2026. Enquanto o mundo assistia a uma possível escalada convencional, o Irã surpreendeu ao acionar sua arma mais letal — o gargalo por onde escoa cerca de 20% do petróleo mundial — transformando um ponto estratégico em instrumento de asfixia econômica global. Com a análise contundente de Robinson Farinazzo, vamos entender como essa estratégia de guerra assimétrica não apenas neutralizou a superioridade militar americana na região, mas também expôs a fragilidade da cadeia de suprimentos ocidental, elevando o preço do barril e forçando potências mundiais a repensarem suas alianças. Descubra os bastidores dessa decisão, os riscos de uma conflagração maior e como o Irã, ao fechar a torneira do Golfo Pérsico, redefiniu as regras do conflito e desafiou a hegemonia dos Estados Unidos no Oriente Médio de forma avassaladora. Esta é uma aula de geopolítica que você não pode perder.

videos.description:

00:00:10 - Início

01:28:50 - Perguntas da audiência

O regime sionista parece ter uma impressionante superioridade militar diante de seus inimigos. Mas será suficiente para neutralizar as contradições internas e o isolamento internacional?

Aumenta cada vez mais a repressão contra os palestinos. Além do genocídio em Gaza e da violência na Cisjordânia e Jerusalém, agora o parlamento aprovou a aplicação da pena de morte apenas para palestinos. O que esconde esse medida tão drástica e racista?

Nesta live imperdível, Breno e Tom Altman vão abordar esse cenário em todos os seus detalhes.

QUAIS OS SINAIS de que a estrutura que sustentou Israel por décadas está se desintegrando?

POR QUE o apoio ocidental — antes incondicional — começa a mostrar rachaduras?

COMO as alianças regionais se reconfiguraram para isolar Tel Aviv?

QUAL É O PAPEL da guerra contra o Irã na aceleração desse processo histórico?

Trata-se de analisar fatos concretos, tendências estruturais e movimentos subterrâneos que a grande mídia prefere ignorar.

O que está em jogo não é apenas o destino de um país, mas a reconfiguração completa do Oriente Médio — e, com ele, de todo o sistema de alianças globais.

🔴 AO VIVO AGORA para uma análise sem meias-palavras. Prepare-se para ouvir o que os telejornais não mostram.

Participe! Envie sua pergunta nos comentários. Breno e Tom respondem ao vivo.

videos.title: Haverá uma invasão terrestre do Irã? (com o Coronel Larry Wilkerson) | The Chris Hedges Report
videos.description:
Enquanto os EUA se preparam para intensificar a guerra contra o Irã com uma possível invasão terrestre, o Coronel Wilkerson revela a longa relutância dos planejadores militares americanos em confrontar o Irã e a insensatez de tal empreendimento.

Apoie meu jornalismo independente no Substack: https://chrishedges.substack.com/

Siga o The Chris Hedges Report nas redes sociais: https://linktr.ee/chrishedges
videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição The contradictory statements emanating from the Trump White House about the war on Iran are evidence of panic. This panic caused by the inability to halt Iranian attacks, open the straight of Hormuz, or cope with a mounting global financial crisis means the Trump administration is now considering conducting ground operations, perhaps attempting to seize the Persian Gulf Islands of Abu Musa and the greater and lesser tombs, Lark and Car Islands. The United States has deployed the Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group with the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit composed of about 3,500 sailors and marines in addition to transport and strike fighter aircraft as well as amphibious assault and tactical assets. It has deployed the boxer amphibious ready group with about 2500 Marines equipped with F35B lightning 2 stealth fighters, MV22B Osprey tilt rotors and attack helicopters. The US has also deployed 2,000 paratroopers of the first brigade combat team of the 82nd Airborne and is reportedly considering augmenting these forces with another 10,000 troops. What will a ground attack look like? How risky will be air or amphibious assaults? Will these assaults result in large casualty rates? Can they force Iran to capitulate? Will they reopen the straight of Hormuz to international shipping? What will such an assault, especially on Carg Island, which lies 16 miles off Iran's coast and processes 90% of the country's oil exports mean for the global economy? Or will a ground war rather than end the conflict widen and prolong it? Will we find ourselves sucked into a quagmire as we did in Iraq and Afghanistan? One that perhaps will end in humiliating defeat. If US troops land on one or more of these Iranian islands, how effective will Iran's anti-ship cruise missiles, ballistic missiles, underwater drones, and mines be in thwarting an assault or an occupation? The Abraham Lincoln carrier strike group has so far stayed out of range of Iran and operates in the Arabian Sea. Could it move closer to support an amphibious assault? Is an amphibious assault so risky that the only way to take these islands will be from the air? And if US forces did occupy one or more of these islands, what kind of damage could Iran's surfaceto-air missiles and air defense drones inflict? How would US forces on these islands obtain logistical support given the difficulty of maneuvering in the narrow straight? Joining me to discuss what may be the next stage of the war on Iran and its potential consequences is Lawrence Wilkerson, a retired Army Colonel and the former chief of staff to Secretary of State Colon Powell. He is a Vietnam War veteran who attended airborne school, Ranger School, and the Naval War College, and who is a helicopter pilot in Vietnam, logged over 1,000 hours on combat missions. He went on to serve as deputy director of the Marine Corps War College at Quantico, and was executive assistant to Admiral Steuart A. Ring, United States Navy Pacific Command, and Director of the United States Marine Corps War College. He has taught at the College of William and Mary and George Washington University. He is a senior fellow at the Eisenhower Media Network, a group of former military, intelligence, and civilian national security officials who describe themselves as offering alternative analyses untainted by Pentagon or defense industry ties and countering Washington's establishment narrative on most national security issues of the day. So before we begin with some of the specific questions that I raised in the introduction, just give us your view, Larry, of where we are with this war. That's difficult to do because the president and the secretary of war defense have not been very articulate in expressing what their mission is and what their purpose is. But let me back up for a moment and paint a little geostrategic landscape that I think is behind this war and is not talked about by many people primarily I think because they don't understand it historically or currently. We really are for some reason actually trying to stop China from executing its probably potentially at least most dangerous space road initiative to our interest. Now that assumes that it is enimical to our interest. I think we have made that assumption. That road would come up through Iran and trace Alexander the Great's route all the way up through the what would be the 1875 or so miles of shoreline on the Persian Gulf on the eastern side and all the way up to the Caucases. It would link China's Pacific ports just as the other three base road initiative railroads do through Russia and the Caucases with Europe in a way that would reduce its shipping time from something like a day and a half to two days depending on which way you go to about 16 hours. Trains have already pied these railroads including the one going through the southern part of Iran. If that happens, then all maritime nations, leading of which is the United States, of course, the protector of the seas, will no longer have seas really worth protecting in the sense of commerce because commerce will be basically landorn. At least 60% of it will come out of Asia and go to Europe and elsewhere on the land. Uh you'll have to get on some kind of boat to get to South America, I guess. But nonetheless, this is a monumental change and it's one we're fighting tooth and nail. Whether Donald Trump knows any of this or Pete Hathth knows any of this, I have no idea. But that is, I think, behind a lot of what we're doing. It's also behind Ukraine because Ukraine stopped the very central railroad which was already making 16-hour trips into the heart of Europe going all the way to Brimavan and La Hav and other places like that critical to European trade if it were not dumbbraided as it were by the war going on in Ukraine. So, and and think about that for a moment as to why Joe Biden and others Blinkin and Sullivan might have had some reason for doing Ukraine as it were rather than what they've been telling us or what they did tell us. This is all about China and it's all about stopping China. Which is to say, I'm not sure that any of this administration that is functioning in front of us every day knows anything about this other than that their masters have told them to do this. and we can talk about who their masters are back in the shadows, but I think that's ultimately what we're looking at. So, it's much more consequential in this struggle between the rising power and the declining power, and there's no question about that now, than people think or know. videos.title: VOCÊ ESTÁ ASSISTINDO O FIM DO MUNDO EM TEMPO REAL (DANIEL LOPEZ) | Crypto Never Sleeps #44
videos.description:
Estamos entrando na maior crise global da história? É o começo de uma nova ordem mundial?

Neste episódio do Crypto Never Sleeps, Daniel Lopez explica como guerras, energia, dólar e tecnologia podem estar conectados em um possível “reset” do sistema global — e o que isso significa para seus investimentos.

O que você vai aprender:
-Existe um plano por trás das guerras atuais?
-O risco real de uma crise global (e até guerra mundial)
-Como o dólar e o sistema financeiro podem mudar
-O papel das stablecoins e do Bitcoin
-O que pode acontecer com comida, energia e mercados
-Como se proteger financeiramente

📌 Inscreva-se no canal e ative as notificações para não perder nenhum episódio!

📩 Newsletter semanal assinada pelo nosso analista Marcello Cestari com todas as principais notícias do mercado de criptomoedas.
https://www.linkedin.com/build-relati...

📢 Anuncie sua marca no Crypto Never Sleeps: comercial@mmakers.com.br


- - - - - - - -


#44 | DANIEL LOPEZ: O COMEÇO DE UMA NOVA ORDEM MUNDIAL
Apresentadores: Marcello Cestari (especialista em criptoativos) e Valter Rebelo (especialista em criptoativos)
Convidado: Daniel Lopez (Jornalista, Professor e Pastor)

#GUERRA #NOVAORDEMMUNDIAL #BITCOIN #INVESTIMENTOS #MERCADOFINANCEIRO #MARKETMAKERS #SATOSHINAKAMOTO

videos.title: A PERTURBADORA VERDADE POR TRÁS DO CASO EPSTEIN | Market Makers #322
videos.description:
O Caso Epstein voltou ao centro do debate — mas quase sempre pelo ângulo errado. Neste episódio, Thiago Salomão conversa com Daniel Lopes para entender o que os “arquivos”, as conexões e a narrativa revelam sobre poder, dinheiro, influência e controle.

Neste episódio, você vai ver:
-Por que o Caso Epstein continua voltando anos depois
-O que é “hangout limitado” e como narrativas são controladas
-A conexão entre mercado financeiro, poder político e reputação
-“Kompromat ”: quando informação vira instrumento de controle
-O que dá (e o que não dá) para concluir quando nomes aparecem em documentos

Qual parte do caso você acha mais importante: dinheiro, mídia ou política?

videos.title: Como Era Viver Dentro de uma Casa na Pompeia em 79 d.C. | Reconstrução com IA
videos.description:
Como era viver dentro de uma casa em Pompeia antes da erupção do Vesúvio em 79 d.C.? Neste documentário, recriamos com Inteligência Artificial os ambientes de uma autêntica domus romana: o atrium com sua piscina central, os afrescos vibrantes nas paredes, a cozinha onde escravos preparavam refeições sobre brasas, o triclinium onde se comia reclinado, os animais domésticos que circulavam pelos jardins e até os grafites que os pompeianos rabiscavam nos muros como uma antiga rede social. Uma simulação imersiva baseada em décadas de pesquisas arqueológicas que revela como viviam, comiam, dormiam e se comunicavam os habitantes de uma das cidades mais fascinantes da história.

Se você gosta de história e reconstruções com IA, CURTA este vídeo, INSCREVA-SE no canal e ATIVE o sino para não perder os próximos episódios da série Reconstrução com IA!

Deixe nos comentários: Qual lugar antigo você quer ver reconstruído no próximo vídeo?

videos.title: A Vida Cotidiana na Roma Antiga em 117 d.C.: Como REALMENTE Era
videos.description:
Viaje para o ano 117 d.C. e acompanhe um dia comum na rotina de quem realmente sustentava Roma: trabalhadores, vendedores, artesãos, mães, escravizados e pequenos comerciantes. Nada de palácios ou decisões do Senado — aqui, a narrativa gira em torno da vida real nas ruas movimentadas da maior cidade do mundo antigo.
Nesta jornada, você verá:
• Como era acordar antes do amanhecer em prédios altos e inseguros
• A pressão do trabalho físico intenso e a ausência de direitos trabalhistas
• Os alimentos simples que compunham a dieta popular
• As termas como espaço de higiene, negócios e interação social
• Os perigos constantes de incêndios, epidemias e violência urbana
• O que a experiência romana revela sobre desigualdade e sobrevivência nas grandes cidades de hoje
Nota sobre as imagens:
As cenas apresentadas foram produzidas com o auxílio de inteligência artificial para representar visualmente a Roma antiga com base em estudos históricos. Como não existem registros visuais diretos do período, as imagens são reconstruções fundamentadas em evidências arqueológicas, literatura clássica e pesquisas acadêmicas reconhecidas. O objetivo é oferecer uma visualização educativa e acessível do cotidiano romano.
Fontes de referência incluem:
• Escavações arqueológicas em cidades preservadas como Pompeia e Herculano
• Escritos de autores romanos da Antiguidade
• Estudos modernos sobre estrutura social, economia e cultura urbana romana
• Artefatos e registros preservados em museus e centros de pesquisa
Apesar do compromisso com rigor histórico, algumas representações envolvem interpretações baseadas nas melhores evidências disponíveis. A vida em Roma variava amplamente conforme classe social, ocupação e contexto local.
Por que olhar para isso hoje?
A história não é construída apenas por líderes e generais, mas por pessoas comuns que enfrentaram desafios diários para sobreviver. Entender o cotidiano dessas pessoas é compreender as bases reais de uma civilização — e reconhecer padrões que ainda moldam nossas sociedades atuais.

videos.title: A REALIDADE BRUTAL da Última Marcha de um Soldado Romano para Casa em 117 d.C – Como REALMENTE Era
videos.description:
Esqueça as estátuas de mármore e os desfiles triunfais. Este documentário reconstrói a jornada real de um veterano do Império Romano no ano da morte de Trajano. Acompanhamos a trajetória de "Marco", um soldado comum que sobreviveu às fronteiras do Danúbio e agora enfrenta o desafio mais difícil: voltar para uma casa que mudou tanto quanto ele.
Mais do que uma narrativa de viagem, esta é uma análise sobre o custo humano do serviço militar antigo. Investigamos as sequelas físicas, o trauma psicológico não nomeado, a burocracia fria da dispensa e o isolamento social de quem dedicou a juventude à águia de Roma.

Neste episódio, você vai conferir:
🔹 O preço do corpo: Lesões crônicas, desgaste articular e a medicina militar da época
🔹 Saúde mental antiga: Evidências históricas do que hoje chamaríamos de estresse pós-traumático
🔹 A dispensa administrativa: O fim do contrato sem cerimônias, apenas papelada e pagamentos
🔹 Logística de retorno: Estradas, estações de parada e a travessia do Adriático
🔹 Isolamento social: A proibição do casamento durante o serviço e o abismo com a família
🔹 Mudanças na terra natal: O declínio do pequeno agricultor e a ascensão das grandes villae
🔹 O reencontro: A chegada silenciosa e o contraste com a propaganda imperial

Uma produção baseada em evidências epigráficas, tratados militares e estudos acadêmicos recentes sobre a condição do veterano na Antiguidade.

🔔 INSCREVA-SE NO CANAL para mais documentários que buscam a história humana por trás das datas e batalhas.
👍 DEIXE SEU LIKE se você acredita que a verdadeira história está na experiência individual, não apenas nos grandes eventos.
💬 COMENTE: Você considera que a "recompensa" dada aos veteranos romanos era justa pelo tempo e saúde entregues ao Império?

📚 FONTES E REFERÊNCIAS BIBLIOGRÁFICAS:
Fontes Primárias:
• VEGETIUS, Flavius. Epitoma Rei Militaris (Tratado sobre assuntos militares).
• GALENO de Pérgamo. Textos médicos sobre lesões e tendões.
• TÁCITO, Públio Cornélio. Anais e Histórias (Relatos sobre veteranos).
• LÍVIO, Tito. Ab Urbe Condita (História de Roma).
• APIANO de Alexandria. Guerras Civis (Caso de Céstio Macedônico).
• COLUMELA, Lúcio Júnio Moderato. De Re Rustica (Sobre a agricultura e villae).

Estudos Acadêmicos e Modernos:
• CAMPBELL, Brian. The Roman Army, 31 BC–AD 337. Routledge.
• Classical Association. "A Roman PTSD? Psychological Trauma and the Soldiers of Rome". 2016.
• Pesquisas sobre honesta missio e diploma militare.
• Estudos sobre o cursus publicus e sistema de mansiones.

🏛️ Capítulos do Vídeo: A Realidade Brutal da Última Marcha

0:00 — Introdução: O Início do Fim para Napoleão
0:45 — A Saída de Moscou: O Exército Carregado de Saques

2:15 — Fome e Desespero: A Dieta de Cavalos e Neve

4:05 — A Batalha de Vyazma: O Colapso da Retaguarda Francesa
5:00 — O Horror de Smolensk: A Esperança que Virou Pesadelo

videos.title: A REALIDADE BRUTAL da Vigia Noturna de um Soldado na Roma Antiga em 117 d.C. Como REALMENTE Era
videos.description:
Entre duas e três da manhã, quando o mundo parece parar de fazer sentido, o que mantém um homem de pé? 🌙🪖
Este documentário não é sobre batalhas épicas ou generais gloriosos. É sobre o momento mais silencioso e mais humano da vida militar romana: a vigia noturna na fronteira do Danúbio, no ano em que Adriano consolidou o Império. Acompanhamos a experiência de um legionário comum, não como símbolo, mas como pessoa — com frio, dúvidas e a necessidade profunda de deixar algum rastro de que existiu.
Mais do que reconstruir uma noite, esta é uma reflexão sobre resistência, isolamento e o que acontece quando um ser humano é inserido num sistema que exige tudo, mas raramente reconhece. Uma narrativa baseada em evidências arqueológicas, tratados militares e fontes literárias que sobreviveram para contar o que os monumentos silenciaram.

Neste episódio, você vai explorar:
🔹 Vigiliae: Como funcionava o sistema de turnos noturnos e por que a precisão era questão de vida ou morte
🔹 A tessera: A tabuinha de madeira que materializava confiança num império de milhões
🔹 Privação de sono: O que Galeno observou clinicamente sobre soldados em serviço noturno prolongado
🔹 Frio e isolamento: A experiência sensorial da guarda na muralha — e como o corpo aprende a suportar
🔹 Tabletes de Vindolanda: Cartas reais que revelam saudade, cansaço e a humanidade por trás da farda
🔹 "Vigilavi": A inscrição anônima em pedra que diz mais sobre resistência do que qualquer discurso oficial
🔹 Fronteira como rotina: Por que ocupar era mais desgastante do que conquistar
🔹 O peso invisível: O que mantinha um homem acordado quando o regulamento não era suficiente

Uma produção para quem acredita que a verdadeira história está nas frestas — nos objetos pequenos, nas palavras riscadas, nos silêncios que o tempo não apagou completamente.

🔔 INSCREVA-SE NO CANAL para mais documentários que buscam a história humana por trás das instituições e dos monumentos.
👍 DEIXE SEU LIKE se você acredita que compreender o passado comum é tão importante quanto estudar os grandes eventos.
💬 COMENTE: Você já passou por uma noite em que a única coisa que o mantinha de pé era a necessidade de provar para si mesmo que estava cumprindo seu papel? Como foi?

📚 FONTES E REFERÊNCIAS BIBLIOGRÁFICAS:
Fontes primárias:
• POLÍBIO de Megalópolis. Histórias, Livro VI — organização militar e sistema de vigias.
• GALENO de Pérgamo. Textos médicos — observações sobre saúde militar e efeitos da privação de sono.
• VEGETIUS, Flavius. Epitoma Rei Militaris — doutrina, rotina e controle no exército imperial.
• Tabulae Vindolandenses — correspondência privada de soldados no forte da Britânia.
• Inscrições de fortes romanos na Muralha de Adriano (Housesteads, Viminacium).

Estudos acadêmicos:
• GOLDSWORTHY, Adrian. The Complete Roman Army. Thames & Hudson, 2003.
• SOUTHERN, Pat. The Roman Army: A Social and Institutional History. Routledge, 2006.
• CAMPBELL, Brian. The Roman Army, 31 BC–AD 337. Routledge, 1994.
• ROTH, Jonathan. The Logistics of the Roman Army at War. Brill, 1999.

Capítulos
0:00 — Introdução: O perigo que começa quando o sol se põe
0:40 — A Escuridão Total: Como era a noite antes da eletricidade
1:25 — Os Vigias Noturnos: A primeira linha de defesa das cidades
2:10 — O Equipamento: Lanternas de óleo e armas de defesa
3:05 — Inimigos Invisíveis: O crime e o medo nos becos medievais
3:55 — O Som do Alerta: Sinos e gritos que cortavam o silêncio
4:42 — O Fogo: O maior pesadelo de uma cidade adormecida
5:30 — Criaturas da Noite: O folclore e a superstição dos guardas
6:18 — A Solidão Brutal: O cansaço e o frio extremo da vigia
7:05 — Corrupção e Suborno: Quando o vigia fechava os olhos
8:12 — O Amanhecer: O alívio de sobreviver a mais uma noite
9:00 — Legado: Como a vigia noturna deu origem à polícia moderna
9:45 — Conclusão e Reflexão Final
